summary: Recent changes in the Firefox browser have focused on enhancing the WebRTC (the technology behind video calls) connection transport layer, specifically related to ICE (Interactive Connectivity Establishment) and DTLS (Datagram Transport Layer Security). Notably, improvements include the implementation of detailed statistics such as TLS version and cipher information for secure communications, which helps in monitoring and optimizing the performance of these connections. Additionally, enhancements have been made to the RTCIceCandidateStats, including new fields that give insight into network connectivity, thereby making WebRTC connections more resilient and efficient. These upgrades are essential as they not only strengthen the security and reliability of real-time communications but also align Firefox with modern web standards, ensuring a better user experience. **Overall, these developments bring more robustness and transparency to secure real-time communication, which is crucial for maintaining the integrity of user data.**
